author: Dan Seaman
description: Kanban view over markdown-based project backlog and milestones. Reads bold-key metadata directly, lets you drag cards between status columns, edit enums inline, and writes changes back - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Dan Seaman
manifest.description: Non-destructive Kanban view over markdown-based project backlog and milestones. Reads bold-key and YAML metadata directly, lets you drag cards between status columns, edit enums inline, and writes changes back without touching the rest of the file.
manifest.name: Kandyban

name: Kandyban

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description:
- Require Obsidian 1.7.2 or newer, matching the workspace APIs Kandyban already uses to open its views reliably.

Builds on 0.5.0–0.5.2: SweetClaude 4.x (YAML frontmatter) support with non-destructive write-back, automatic format detection, and popout-window-safe views.

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description:
- Better behaviour when an Obsidian window is popped out: the board and list now use the active window's document and cross-window-safe type checks, so popovers and inline edits work correctly in detached windows.

Builds on 0.5.0–0.5.1: SweetClaude 4.x (YAML frontmatter) support with non-destructive write-back and automatic format detection.

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description:
- Trim the plugin description to fit Obsidian's 250-character limit so installs and updates no longer error.

Includes everything from 0.5.0: SweetClaude 4.x (YAML frontmatter) support for issues, stories, and epics, with non-destructive write-back and automatic format detection.

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description:
- Kandyban now reads SweetClaude 4.x projects, where each item's details live in a YAML block at the top of the file (issues, stories, and epics). Your board and list fill in just like they always have — no settings to change.
- Dragging a card or cycling a value writes the change back into that YAML block and leaves the rest of the file exactly as it was, the same careful, non-destructive edit Kandyban already did for older projects.
- Epics are treated as groupings rather than tasks: they don't clutter the board as cards, and the items belonging to them show their epic as a tag.
- A new **Roadmap path** setting (default `product/roadmap`) so epics under `roadmap/epics/` are picked up.
- Older SweetClaude projects keep working exactly as before — Kandyban detects each file's format automatically.

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description:
- Open the list view straight from the left ribbon — a new checklist icon, no keyboard shortcut needed.
- Board cards are now ordered by horizon within each column (soonest first), so what's most pressing rises to the top.

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description:
- Milestones now appear in the **Milestone** column, and you can filter the board and list by milestone.
- Milestone files no longer show up as if they were tasks in the list.

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description:
List view: sortable columns and multi-select filters.

- Click any column header to sort: asc → desc → none. Status and horizon sort by configured vocabulary order; id/milestone use natural sort; title/scope use locale compare. Missing values always sort to the end.
- Filter status, horizon, and milestone via a popover with checkboxes (e.g. "all statuses except done"). Tri-state model: no filter, include list, or explicitly empty. Popover stays open across edits.
- Filter and sort state reset when the view is closed.

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description:
Show effort estimate chip on board cards.

- Parser surfaces `Effort` from the bold-key block into `enums.effort` (annotations stripped, lowercased).
- Board cards render an effort chip next to milestone and horizon when present. Monospace + uppercase so xs/s/m/l/xl reads as a size tag.

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description:
## Always render all configured status columns

Previously, configured statuses with no matching items were hidden, which made the board hop around as columns appeared and disappeared. Every entry in your `Status enums` setting now renders as a column regardless of whether any items currently occupy it. Unexpected statuses found in data still trail alphabetically.

This makes the configured order predictable — you can put `backlog, in-progress, done, deferred` in settings and the board will always show those four columns left-to-right, even on a brand-new project with no items.

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description:
## Fix: detect SweetClaude state via `sweetclaude.yaml`

SweetClaude v3.18+ consolidates `phase.yaml` + `skills.yaml` into a single `sweetclaude.yaml`. Kandyban previously probed only `phase.yaml`, so projects on the unified format were misread as having no SweetClaude project at all.

- `detectMode()` now probes `sweetclaude.yaml` first, falling back to `phase.yaml` for older projects.
- `refreshMode()` treats `sweetclaude.yaml` as the only source of truth when present; `phase.yaml` is consulted only when the unified file is absent.
- The modify-event watcher fires for either file.
- Settings tab intro and flow-mode placeholder copy updated to reflect the canonical filename.

No behavior change for projects that haven't migrated yet.

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description: 0.2.5 shipped with a lint failure on the settings intro paragraph. 0.2.6 rewords the copy to satisfy the sentence-case rule. No behavior change.
releases.name: Kandyban 0.2.6 — settings intro lint fix

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description: Fixes the settings tab's intro paragraph: it was still referencing the old plugin name (Kansidian) and asserting the vault must be `.sweetclaude/` (no longer true since 0.2.0 — both legacy and project-root layouts are supported). The new intro reads the detected vault mode and explains what Kandyban is looking at.
releases.name: Kandyban 0.2.5 — settings tab fixes

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description:
Renames the plugin from Kansidian to Kandyban (id and display name both change). Pivots the identity away from SweetClaude (no longer in the name) and resolves the Obsidian community directory's fuzzy-match rejection of "Kansidian" (too close to "Obsidian").

Internal architecture and behavior unchanged from 0.2.3. Same 25 KB bundle, same vault layouts supported, same byte-identity write contract.

## Upgrading from a previous build

The id change means the plugin folder path changes from `.obsidian/plugins/kansidian/` to `.obsidian/plugins/kandyban/`. To upgrade:

- BRAT users: remove and re-add the beta plugin (BRAT picks up the new id automatically on fresh install)
- Manual / symlink users: delete the old `kansidian/` plugin folder and create a fresh `kandyban/` folder with the new assets

Settings stored under the old id won't carry over — re-enter the few scan paths and enum vocabularies after reinstalling.
releases.name: Kandyban 0.2.4 — plugin rename

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description:
Addresses the two automated warnings from Obsidian's community plugin directory:

- `authorUrl` corrected to point to the personal GitHub profile, not the repo.
- README H1 now matches the manifest display name ("SweetClaude Kanban").

No code changes from 0.2.2.
releases.name: Kansidian 0.2.3 — directory warning fixes

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description: Renames the manifest display name from "Kansidian" to "SweetClaude Kanban" so the Obsidian community directory accepts the submission. No code changes from 0.2.1; same bundle.
releases.name: Kansidian 0.2.2 — display name for community store

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description:
Drops the parenthetical from the display name (now just **Kansidian**, not **Kansidian (SweetClaude Kanban)**) so the Obsidian community directory accepts the submission. The SweetClaude / Kanban framing stays in the manifest description and the README.

No code changes. Same 25.6 KB bundle as 0.2.0.

## Upgrading

BRAT users: auto-picks the new version next time it checks. Manual install: replace the three files with this release's assets.
releases.name: Kansidian 0.2.1 — name fix for community store

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description:
Adds support for opening the project root as your Obsidian vault (in addition to the original `.sweetclaude/`-as-vault mode). Kansidian auto-detects which layout you're using and adapts.

## What's new in 0.2.0

- **Project-root vault layout.** Open your project root as the Obsidian vault — Kansidian finds `.sweetclaude/` automatically via `vault.adapter`. Each project's vault now has its own distinguishable name in Obsidian's switcher (instead of every project showing as "sweetclaude").
- **Hybrid auto-detection.** Both layouts are supported; no settings to configure. Kansidian decides on plugin load by checking which layout's `state/phase.yaml` exists.
- **Soft hidden-files plugin detection.** In project-root mode, Kansidian detects whether a community "Show Hidden Files" plugin is installed. If yes, click-to-open lands in Obsidian. If no, it falls back to your system's default markdown handler; a one-time Notice nudges you toward the plugin if you want the upgraded experience.
- **`isDesktopOnly: true`** — declared honestly now that we use Electron's `shell.openPath()` for the click-to-open fallback.

## Background

0.1.0 assumed the vault root was always `.sweetclaude/`. Two real-world issues surfaced:
- Multiple SweetClaude projects all showed as "sweetclaude" in the vault switcher (indistinguishable).
- Obsidian's "Rename vault" action moves the directory on disk — renaming a `.sweetclaude/`-based vault breaks every SweetClaude tool that hardcodes the path.

0.2.0 resolves both by supporting the project-root layout as an equally first-class option.

See [README — Vault setup](https://github.com/dseaman/Kansidian/blob/master/README.md#vault-setup) for the full layout matrix and trade-offs.

## Compatibility

- Obsidian 1.5.0+
- Desktop only
- Backwards-compatible with 0.1.0 installs — no migration needed; existing `.sweetclaude/`-as-vault users keep their current setup with zero changes.

## Install

Same three assets as 0.1.0: `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`. Drop them in `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/kansidian/` (path depends on which layout you choose — see README).
releases.name: Kansidian 0.2.0 — vault flexibility

releases.author: dseaman
releases.description:
First tagged release of Kansidian — a non-destructive Kanban view over SweetClaude project work, inside Obsidian.

**Status:** pre-alpha. Verified against a real SweetClaude vault (~135 items). Not yet community-store ready.

## What's in this build

- **Board view (kanban).** Drag cards between status columns. Native HTML5 drag-and-drop with animated card transitions and a destination flash so the eye lands at the change. Cards show id, title, milestone, horizon chip.
- **List view (table).** id, title, status, horizon, milestone, scope. Click id/title to open the underlying file in a new tab; click status/horizon for an inline picker.
- **Non-destructive writes.** Annotations like `done (merged 2026-05-19, PR #29)` survive enum changes byte-identical. Only the leading enum portion of the `**Field:** …` line is spliced; everything else stays untouched.
- **Mode-aware.** Reads `mode` from `state/phase.yaml`. Kanban and agile modes render normally; flow and Shape Up modes show explanatory placeholders. Unset mode (e.g. for projects that haven't set a mode field) renders normally.
- **Search + filter dropdowns.** Status, horizon, milestone. Search focus/cursor preserved across re-renders. ✕ button clears.
- **Dual convention support.** Reads both `I-*` (current SweetClaude) and `BL-*` (legacy) work items. Recognizes `**Horizon:**` and `**Priority:**` as the same field.
- **Ribbon icon.** One-click access to the board.
- **Cycle commands.** `Cycle status forward` and `Cycle horizon forward` for hotkey binding.
- **Settings tab.** Configurable scan paths (backlog, issues, milestones) and enum vocabularies.
- **Rescan command** with a built-in hint about SweetClaude's cache-staleness behavior.

## Test coverage

33 vitest cases including the load-bearing round-trip byte-identity assertion on parser/writer.

## Compatibility

- Obsidian 1.5.0+
- Desktop tested (macOS verified)
- SweetClaude 3.68.6 verified

## Install (manual)

Download the three assets attached to this release. Place them in `<your-project>/.sweetclaude/.obsidian/plugins/kansidian/`. Open `.sweetclaude/` as a vault in Obsidian (use `⌘+Shift+.` in the file picker to reveal hidden folders). Enable Kansidian in Community Plugins.

See the [README](https://github.com/dseaman/Kansidian/blob/master/README.md) for full installation, usage, settings reference, mode support matrix, and troubleshooting docs.

## Known limitations

- No support for SweetClaude native types beyond `I-*`, `BL-*`, `MS-*` (epics, themes, sprints, pitches, cycles deferred).
- No item creation from inside Kansidian — use `/sweetclaude:project-issues new` for now.
- Desktop-only tested.
- SweetClaude's cached `session-status.txt` doesn't auto-refresh on Obsidian-driven edits; the **Rescan vault** command explains how to refresh manually.
releases.name: Kansidian 0.1.0 — pre-alpha
